events.description: Detecting 50 failed login attempts from a single IP address (192.168.1.100) indicates a high probability of a brute force or credential stuffing attack targeting user accounts.
events.action: Block the source IP address (192.168.1.100) immediately at the firewall or WAF, verify if any attempts succeeded, and ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enforced on all accounts.

events.description: An internal IP (10.0.0.55) downloaded an unusually large volume of data (15.5 GB), indicating potential unauthorized data staging, insider threat, or a compromised host.
events.action: Isolate the host at 10.0.0.55 from the network, identify the data source, and review active sessions and privileges of the associated user account.

events.description: An internal host (172.16.0.200) performed a rapid port scan of 1000 ports, indicating potential host compromise and lateral movement reconnaissance within the cloud environment.
events.action: Isolate the instance with IP 172.16.0.200 immediately using security groups, revoke active credentials, and initiate an incident response investigation.
